rationale: In the theory development section, the citation is used to support the claim that consumer responses depend on the relationship between competing brands, not just isolated brand attributes. The authors specifically reference the 2014 result that small brands gain support when framed against larger competitors, then argue they build on this logic. This helps position their own hypotheses about rival versus nonrival references in public messages, so the citation has a clear conceptual role beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Paharia, Avery, and Keinan (2014) as prior evidence that interbrand relational context can shift consumer support. It extends that idea to a different mechanism and context by testing how historically grounded rivalry references in brand communications affect engagement and downstream purchase intentions.
